description_long: This object is a wooden picket fence section, featuring evenly spaced vertical slats with pointed tops and two horizontal rails for structural support. The fence is constructed from light-colored wood with a visible grain pattern, giving it a natural and decorative appearance. One end of the fence is anchored by a thick, square post, which is more robust than the slats and provides stability. The pointed tops of the slats are designed for both aesthetic appeal and to deter climbing. The wood appears to be smoothly finished, with no visible signs of wear or weathering. This type of fence is commonly used in residential neighborhoods, gardens, or parks to demarcate boundaries while maintaining an inviting look.
description: A light wood picket fence section with pointed slats and a sturdy square post, featuring a smooth, natural finish. Ideal for decorative boundary marking in residential or garden settings.
description_view_0: A narrow side view showing the thickness of the fence and the square post, with the pointed tops of the slats barely visible.
description_view_1: A frontal view displaying the evenly spaced pointed slats, two horizontal rails, and the prominent square post on the left.
description_view_2: A side profile emphasizing the slimness of the fence and the alignment of the slats, with the post visible at one end.
description_view_3: A rear view showing the horizontal rails more clearly, the pointed slats, and the square post on the right.
